region_of_origin: Singapore (served by CHICHA San Chen, a Taiwanese-origin tea and dessert brand)

lemon curd filling (lemon juice, lemon zest, eggs, sugar, butter, cream)

serving.description: 1 individual tart slice or whole mini tart
serving.natural_language_portion: One individual creamy lemon tart as served at CHICHA San Chen, approximately 100 g, representing a single-portion dessert item.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon meringue pie (as closest analogous pastry-custard tart); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18330; supplemented with USDA Foundation Foods data for shortcrust pastry and lemon curd components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry for 'creamy lemon tart' exists. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy lemon meringue pie (NDB 18330) and USDA Foundation Foods shortcrust pastry and lemon curd components. The creamy lemon tart differs from lemon meringue pie in that it uses a cream-enriched lemon custard filling rather than meringue topping; fat and energy values are adjusted upward slightly to reflect the cream content. This is a medium-confidence estimate. HPB Singapore Foodo was checked and does not contain a specific lemon tart entry.

gi_source.title: Flan/custard tart and shortcrust pastry analogous GI estimate; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed journal (Diabetes Care)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for creamy lemon tart found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous custard tart and shortcrust pastry entries in the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). Custard-based pastry tarts with refined flour shells typically fall in the GI range 50–60. Glycemic load per 100 g serving calculated as (55 × 41.0) / 100 = 22.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (185 mg per 100 g) and saturated fat from butter and cream
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a tart and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the sodium and saturated fat content from the pastry shell and cream filling can contribute to elevated blood pressure with regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content (41 g carbohydrate, 24 g sugar per 100 g) with a low glycemic index of 55 and glycemic load of 22.6 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence only; the high sugar and refined flour content produce a significant glycemic load per serving, and portion control to half a tart with a low-GI meal context is strongly advised to limit postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (9.2 g per 100 g), cholesterol (115 mg per 100 g), and trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g) from butter-rich pastry and cream filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dessert sparingly, as the saturated fat and cholesterol content from butter and cream can adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels; limiting intake to occasional small portions and choosing lower-fat dessert alternatives on most occasions is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Creamy Lemon Tart (又见柠檬塔; 柠檬塔) is a Western-style pastry dessert served in Singapore by CHICHA San Chen, a Taiwanese-origin tea and dessert brand. The tart consists of a baked shortcrust pastry shell made from wheat flour, butter, sugar, and eggs, filled with a rich, cream-enriched lemon custard or lemon curd. The filling is characterised by a smooth, velvety texture with a bright, tangy lemon flavour balanced by the sweetness of sugar and the richness of cream and butter. The tart is typically chilled or set before serving, giving it a firm yet yielding custard consistency. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 325 kcal, 4.8 g protein, 16.5 g fat (of which 9.2 g is saturated), and 41 g carbohydrate including 24 g sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 22.6 per 100 g serving, making it a high-glycemic-load dessert unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing weight. The high saturated fat and cholesterol content from butter and cream are relevant for cardiovascular disease management. Sodium at 185 mg per 100 g is moderate. The tart contains gluten, eggs, and dairy and is not suitable for vegans, those with gluten intolerance, or those with dairy allergies. It is vegetarian. In Singapore's food culture, lemon tarts are popular café and dessert-shop items, and CHICHA San Chen's version is positioned as a premium dessert pairing with their tea beverages.

confidence_reason: No exact match for creamy lemon tart found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy lemon meringue pie (NDB 18330) and component-level data for shortcrust pastry and lemon curd, adjusted for cream enrichment. GI is estimated from analogous custard tart entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Medium confidence reflects the use of analogous and component-based estimation rather than a direct measurement of this specific product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy lemon meringue pie (NDB 18330) as the closest analogous whole-dish entry, with upward adjustment to fat and energy to reflect the cream-enriched filling of this specific product. This is an estimation, not a direct measurement. 2. GI of 55 (low category) is an analogous estimate based on custard tart and shortcrust pastry entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care). 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used for estimation. 6. Halal status is listed as false because CHICHA San Chen's halal certification status in Singapore is not confirmed from an approved source; this should be verified against MUIS records. 7. The food_name in the input JSON ('Creamy Lemon Tart又见柠檬塔') has been normalised to the canonical English form 'Creamy Lemon Tart'; the Chinese name '又见柠檬塔' is preserved in aliases and local_names. 8. Multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi in accordance with the Singaporean dish multilingual requirement, as this dish is served in Singapore. 9. Serving size is set to 100 g for per-100 g nutrient alignment; actual restaurant portion may vary.
